markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's latest delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026—confirmed by Take-Two in earnings calls and official statements—has anchored trader expectations around further pre-release milestones. With summer 2026 marketing now underway, attention centers on a likely third trailer, pre-order launch, and pricing reveal, all typical catalysts that historically precede major Rockstar titles. Industry watchers note the studio's emphasis on polish after two slips from the original Fall 2025 window, while strong fiscal forecasts signal stable internal timelines. Upcoming chart updates, social buzz, and any guild or platform announcements could shift implied probabilities on additional trailers or further adjustments before launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Recent mistrials have sharply shifted trader sentiment toward "No Prison Time."** In May 2026, Harvey Weinstein’s third New York trial on the Jessica Mann rape charge ended in a hung jury, marking the second consecutive deadlock on that count after a 2025 mixed verdict convicted him on one related charge. With prosecutors set to decide by late June whether to pursue a fourth trial, repeated jury reluctance and Weinstein’s advanced age plus documented health issues (cancer, diabetes, cardiac concerns) make additional convictions appear unlikely. The 16-year Los Angeles sentence remains consecutive to any New York time, yet ongoing appeals and the practical limits of enforcing long terms on a 74-year-old inmate reinforce the market’s strong consensus around minimal or no further incarceration. Upcoming rulings on retrial and sentencing timelines are the key near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights has produced a 3-2 series lead for Carolina after five games, directly shaping the current trader consensus. The Hurricanes earned the top seed in the Eastern Conference with 113 regular-season points and advanced through the Senators, Flyers, and Canadiens, showcasing strong depth and goaltending consistency. Vegas, the Western Conference representative, defeated the Avalanche in the conference finals following earlier wins over the Mammoth and Ducks, relying on offensive firepower and resilience in extended series. Recent momentum from Carolina’s back-to-back victories, including a 4-2 Game 5 win, has elevated the team’s implied probability, while Vegas retains realistic comeback potential in the remaining games at T-Mobile Arena and beyond. Series volatility has caused odds to flip multiple times, reflecting the closely matched clubs and inherent uncertainty in a best-of-seven format.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 81.6% implied probability to win the 2026 NBA title because they lead the San Antonio Spurs 3-1 in the Finals following a record 29-point comeback victory in Game 4. That win shifted series momentum sharply after the Spurs had built a commanding lead, leaving New York one victory from its first championship since 1973. The Spurs, who opened the series as betting favorites, now face the steep task of winning three straight games on the road and at home to force a Game 7. Jalen Brunson’s consistent scoring and OG Anunoby’s late-game heroics have anchored New York’s recent form, while San Antonio’s second-half collapse in Game 4 exposed vulnerabilities that traders have priced into the current consensus. This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the World Cup winner market at 16.7% and 16.1% implied probability because both enter the expanded 48-team tournament with elite squad depth and recent major-tournament pedigree. Spain, fresh off its Euro 2024 title, features dynamic attacking options led by Lamine Yamal and has posted dominant qualifying numbers in possession and chance creation. France counters with unmatched talent across positions, including Kylian Mbappé, and a track record of reaching multiple recent finals. Portugal, England, Brazil, and Argentina trail closely as each retains star power and favorable draws, while notable pre-tournament injuries to players such as Neymar, Rodrygo, and several Dutch standouts have tempered expectations for other contenders. The bunched pricing reflects traders pricing in a wide-open group stage and knockout volatility across multiple high-quality European and South American sides.

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of a designated successor, supporting trader expectations that he will remain paramount leader past 2027. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling a precedent-breaking third term in 2022, and analyses indicate he is positioned for a fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress. Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and a planned June visit to North Korea, underscores his active role without signs of internal challenges or health-related constraints. Potential successors remain unpromoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, reinforcing continuity over transition.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus places Gavin Newsom atop the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field at 23 percent amid a fragmented field where no candidate exceeds single digits beyond the leader. Recent profile-raising by prospective contenders—including Newsom’s memoir and AI regulatory positioning, Harris and Ocasio-Cortez visits to Southern states, and governors like Shapiro and Beshear emphasizing statewide wins—has sustained early interest. Primary calendar adjustments and voter priorities in the South, where Black voters have historically shaped nominations, add layers. Support could consolidate through fundraising hauls, endorsements from party figures, or standout performances in early contests, as name recognition currently outweighs tested general-election appeal among many listed options.
